dataSources.title: BIG3
dataSources.description: An open prospective longitudinal cohort in Skåne focusing on three major public health challenges: COPD, cardiovascular disease, and lung cancer. Approximately 100,000 residents aged 45–75 were invited to complete lifestyle and environment questionnaires; a subset underwent detailed examinations including ECG, CT, lung function, and blood sampling. Blood samples are stored in a biobank for research. The cohort supports precision medicine research on risk identification and resilience in cardiopulmonary disease.

dataSources.title: The Swedish CArdioPulmonary BioImage Study (SCAPIS)
dataSources.description: A national population-based cohort that used advanced imaging (e.g. coronary CT, carotid ultrasound), biomarkers, and genetic analyses to study cardiopulmonary disease in ~30,000 participants aged 50–64 across six Swedish university hospitals. It provides rich imaging and molecular data linked with registries, supporting precision medicine approaches for prevention and risk prediction.

dataSources.title: Northern Sweden Health and Disease Study (NSHDS)
dataSources.description: A long-running research infrastructure combining three major cohorts (VIP, MONICA, Mammography Screening Project) with biological samples and extensive health data from >100,000 participants. Enables research on genetic, environmental, and lifestyle factors in chronic disease and supports precision medicine and public health interventions.

dataSources.title: Swedish Twin Registry (Svenska Tvillingregistret)
dataSources.description: One of the world's largest twin registries, with data from identical and fraternal twins. It supports research that separates genetic from environmental effects on health and disease. The resource is used for heritability studies, behavioural and clinical traits, and precision medicine–oriented research.

dataSources.title: LifeGene
dataSources.description: A national prospective population-based cohort with biological samples (blood, serum, urine) and questionnaire data from ~30,000 participants across ages. It enables large-scale studies on gene–environment interactions and common diseases, supporting personalised medicine and public health research.

dataSources.title: Karolinska Mammography Project for Risk Prediction of Breast Cancer (KARMA)
dataSources.description: A prospective cohort of women in mammography screening with mammographic images, blood samples, and detailed questionnaires. KARMA supports development of individualised risk models for breast cancer using density, genetics, and lifestyle, and is a key resource for precision screening and prevention research.

dataSources.title: Uppsala–Umeå Comprehensive Cancer Consortium (U-CAN)
dataSources.description: A cancer biobank and study infrastructure that collects biological samples and clinical data from cancer patients. It supports translational and precision medicine research in cancer diagnostics and treatment, enabling tailored therapy development and outcome studies.

dataSources.title: Swedish Infrastructure for Medical Population-Based Life-Course and Environmental Research (SIMPLER)
dataSources.description: An infrastructure that brings together several Swedish cohorts to facilitate research on life-course and environmental determinants of health. It provides a unified platform for data access and supports multidisciplinary studies on diet, lifestyle, and disease prevention.

dataSources.title: The Environmental Determinants of Diabetes in the Young (TEDDY)
dataSources.description: An international prospective study that follows genetically at-risk children from birth to identify environmental triggers of type 1 diabetes. Swedish sites contribute to this multi-country cohort. TEDDY supports research on early-life factors and potential preventive strategies for type 1 diabetes.

dataSources.title: Epidemiological Investigation of Risk Factors for Multiple Sclerosis (EIMS)
dataSources.description: A nationwide case–control study of environmental and genetic risk factors for multiple sclerosis (MS). It includes MS patients and matched healthy controls with questionnaire and blood sample data. EIMS is a key resource for understanding MS aetiology and for precision medicine–oriented research in neurology.

dataSources.title: Immunomodulation and Multiple Sclerosis Epidemiology study (IMSE)
dataSources.description: A phase IV clinical trial monitoring long-term safety and effectiveness of immunomodulatory treatments for multiple sclerosis (MS). The study explores how demographic and clinical factors affect treatment response and aims to improve understanding of nervous system inflammation and the interplay of genetic and environmental factors in MS onset and progression. Data are collected in collaboration with Swedish MS clinics.

dataSources.title: Human Glioblastoma Cell Cultures (HGCC)
dataSources.description: A biobank of well-characterised glioblastoma cell lines derived from Swedish patients, representing major molecular subtypes. HGCC preserves stem cell-like properties and supports in vitro and in vivo research on tumour biology, genetics, and therapy development. The resource is used for precision medicine–oriented glioblastoma research and drug discovery.

dataSources.title: Malmö förebyggande Medicin (MFM)
dataSources.description: A historical cohort (1974–1991) of health screenings in Malmö that identified risk factors for cardiovascular disease and diabetes. The collection includes biological samples and detailed health information and has been used to develop risk prediction models and inform public health policy. MFM data remain a foundation for longitudinal and registry-linked precision medicine research.

dataSources.title: Malmö Kost Cancer (MKC)
dataSources.description: A cohort initiated in 1991 investigating links between diet, lifestyle, and cancer and other chronic diseases. Dietary and health data support research on nutrition and disease development and have informed dietary guidelines and public health recommendations. MKC is part of the Malmö cohort ecosystem and can be linked with other local and national data for precision medicine research.

dataSources.title: COMBAT-MS
dataSources.description: A study involving Swedish university clinics and Kaiser Permanente Southern California, including individuals with MS who started or switched their first disease-modifying treatment (2011–2018). Data include disease impact, patient-reported outcomes, blood samples, and MRI, largely via the Swedish MS Registry. The project continues under Karolinska Neuro-Immunology & MS Center (KNIMS) and connects to the European MAGNIMS imaging network for research on disease progression and mechanisms.

dataSources.title: The Malmö Offspring Study (MOS)
dataSources.description: An intergenerational cohort studying offspring of participants from earlier Malmö cohorts. MOS examines genetic and environmental contributions to health and disease across generations and supports research on familial disease transmission and early-life interventions for precision medicine and prevention.

dataSources.title: Betydelsen av Migration & Etnicitet för Diabetsutvecklingen i Malmö (MEDIM)
dataSources.description: A study examining how migration and ethnicity influence type 2 diabetes development in Malmö. It addresses health disparities and culturally informed prevention, contributing to precision public health and metabolic disease research in diverse populations.
